description: Full-row highlight for tax (rental/diesel tax under 租税公課) and undetermined account (未確定勘定) entries in Google Sheets output

userStories.title: Full-row highlight for 租税公課 entries containing 宿泊税 or 軽油税
userStories.description: As an accountant, I want the entire row highlighted in yellow when debit account is 租税公課 and the description contains 宿泊税 or 軽油税, so I can spot these tax entries at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: In anomaly_detector.py, modify the existing tax_review rule (debit_account == '租税公課' block): add 'full_row': true to the flag dict and remove 'col': 8

In sheets_output.py, modify _apply_anomaly_highlight: when flag.get('full_row') is truthy, apply highlight to range A{row}:AB{row} instead of a single cell; otherwise keep existing single-cell logic unchanged
Color remains yellow (severity 'low', Color(1, 1, 0.7)) for the full row
Existing cell-level highlights for other anomaly types (date-empty, vendor-empty, high-amount, etc.) are unaffected
Tests pass: mock entry {'debit_account': '租税公課', 'description': '宿泊税', 'amount': 200} produces a flag with full_row=true and no col field

userStories.title: Full-row highlight for 未確定勘定 entries
userStories.description: As an accountant, I want the entire row highlighted in orange when the debit account is 未確定勘定, so I can immediately see which entries need manual classification.
acceptanceCriteria: In anomaly_detector.py, add new rule: when debit_account == UNKNOWN_ACCOUNT (import from config.py, value '未確定勘定'), append flag with type 'undetermined_account', severity 'medium', full_row true, and no col field
The _apply_anomaly_highlight method (already updated in US-001) applies orange highlight Color(1, 0.9, 0.7) to A{row}:AB{row} for this flag
Triggers for both paths that produce 未確定勘定: empty debit account fallback AND CREDIT_ONLY_ACCOUNTS replacement (both resolved in sheets_output.py before detect_anomalies is called)
Tests pass: mock entry {'debit_account': '未確定勘定', 'amount': 5000} produces a flag with type 'undetermined_account', severity 'medium', full_row true
